analysis.problem: Lack of effective critiques and solutions to address the potential catastrophic risks of AI
analysis.root_cause: Insufficient dialogue and collaboration between experts and stakeholders to identify and mitigate AI-related risks
leverage_points: Research institutions and organizations focused on AI safety and ethics

proposed_actions.action: Establish a global AI safety and ethics forum for experts and stakeholders to share research and solutions
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Increase the number of published research papers and implemented solutions by 20% within the next 12 months

proposed_actions.action: Develop and implement AI safety and ethics education and training programs for policymakers and regulators
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Train at least 500 policymakers and regulators within the next 18 months

evidence: Holden Karnofsky's discussion on the potential negative effects of AI safety efforts
Yoshua Bengio's TED talk on the catastrophic risks of AI and the need for a safer path
The announcement of the Cluelessness Critiques Competition to elicit responses to Anthony DiGiovanni's sequence
analysis.generalization: This problem connects to the domains of civilization, human, and planet, as unmitigated AI risks could have far-reaching consequences for global stability, human well-being, and the environment
analysis.summary: The lack of effective critiques and solutions to address AI risks poses a high-severity problem, requiring urgent attention from research institutions, policymakers, and regulators to establish a global forum for dialogue and solution-sharing

raw_items.title: A brief list of ways AI safety efforts could be net negative
raw_items.summary:

I tend to think it’s worse than 51/49. I tend to think we’re always going to be prone to overestimate how robustly good our actions are. And the more we learn about all the galaxy-brained considerations that one should have had in one’s head, the more it’s going to be like 50+ε%. I think AI safe

raw_items.title: Online >> real life for spreading ideas
raw_items.summary:
I believe that internet culture influences real culture much more than the other way around. This is quite hard to prove, but I often see an idea start to come up in real-life conversations where I’ve seen it appear on Twitter a few weeks before. I’m not sure if ordinary people don’t realise this (that their ideas are workshopped and take root online), or if they just see it as that important.

Furthermore, the internet is still quite meritocratic. Often one random blo

raw_items.title: Typical Minds Aren’t
raw_items.summary:
We all know the typical mind fallacy—the bias where we assume that other people’s minds are much like our own. It happens because most of our evidence for what minds are like comes from experiencing what our own mind is like, and thus we infer from that evidence that the minds of others are not so different from ours.

raw_items.title: San Silvestro
raw_items.summary: I will note that her relationship with the divine was inextricably sexual. Her carnal fantasies she revealed to me, as she revealed all her sins, for I was her confessor. It is in the nature of Man to sin and then sin again. And if they are of our flock, this cycle is unconstrained by repentance, which is to the temporal almost an appurtenance and to the spiritual anything but. raw_items.title: QURI is moving into maintenance mode
raw_items.summary:
Published on June 19, 2026 5:37 PM GMT

After about seven years, QURI is moving into maintenance mode. This means we’ll ensure that key software (Guesstimate and SquiggleHub) is maintained, but we won’t be developing new software or doing new research.

Background
